title: Rousseauxia tamatavensis
intro: Rousseauxia tamatavensis, also known as Madagascar rose, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Melastomataceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Madagascar for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Rousseauxia tamatavensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Rousseauxia tamatavensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It is native to Madagascar. The classification of Rousseauxia tamatavensis is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the subkingdom Angiospermophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Myrtales, and family Melastomataceae. Within the family Melastomataceae, Rousseauxia tamatavensis is placed in the genus Rousseauxia. The species is also known by its synonym Rousseauxia brevifolia.
